title: One Bill Task, Tea and Candle
subtitle: A grounding activity for doing one financial task with care
excerpt: A simple activity for breaking through the avoidance of financial admin. Pick one bill-related task, make a drink, light a candle, and do just that one thing. The ritual around it changes the feel of the task itself.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education, drawing on the Day 53 activity from MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

content.text: The task is already on your list. It has been for a while. This activity is not about tackling everything at once: it's about picking one item and giving it a proper seat at the table.

A small deliberate context (a drink, a candle, a door closed) separates this from the ambient dread that follows admin around. The task is still the task. The frame around it changes how heavy it feels to start.
Identify one specific bill-related task: a direct debit to check, a form to complete, a payment to set up. One item only.
Make a drink you enjoy. Boil the kettle properly; pour it into a cup you like.
Light a candle if you have one nearby. Sit somewhere other than your usual work spot if you can.
Do only the one task. When it's done, close the tab or the document. Do not open another task.
Notice the task is done. Blow out the candle.

The most common resistance here is expanding the session mid-task ('while I'm here I may as well...'). Staying with one item is the practice. Completion of one thing is more useful than a half-done five.
Written for homemade.education. The Day 53 activity in MONEY by Rebecca led the way.
